documents: MCRP 8-10B.1 / MCT 8-10A Marine Corps Task List
NAVMC 3500.84B Health Services Support T&R Manual (referenced METs)
source.notes: Seed catalog; definitions paraphrased from doctrinal taxonomies for tooltip display. Operators should consult the authoritative publication for any compliance-bearing use. Expand as additional METs are encountered during assessments.

mets.title: Deploy/Conduct Maneuver
mets.description: Top-level Marine Corps Task covering deployment and maneuver of forces to position and displace for mission accomplishment.

mets.title: Conduct Force Projection
mets.description: Project combat forces from a home station, staging area, or other location to a designated operational area.

mets.title: Provide Forces
mets.description: Make trained, equipped, and ready forces available to a supported commander to meet an operational requirement.

mets.title: Conduct Amphibious Operations
mets.description: Plan and execute operations launched from the sea by a landing force embarked in ships or craft.

mets.title: Support Amphibious Operations
mets.description: Provide combat, combat support, and combat service support (including HSS) to the amphibious force.

mets.title: Provide Sustainment
mets.description: Top-level Marine Corps Task covering sustainment of the MAGTF, including supply, maintenance, transportation, general engineering, services, and Health Service Support.

mets.title: Provide Health Service Support (HSS)
mets.description: Provide a coordinated system of medical capabilities to promote, protect, and restore the health of the force; deliver combat casualty care across the continuum from point of injury to definitive care.

mets.title: Conduct Casualty Treatment
mets.description: Treat combat and non-combat casualties through initial and resuscitative care, damage control resuscitation, and stabilization for evacuation.

mets.title: Conduct Casualty Triage
mets.description: Sort casualties based on the severity of injury, urgency of treatment, and availability of resources to do the greatest good for the greatest number.

mets.title: Conduct Temporary Casualty Holding
mets.description: Provide short-duration holding of casualties awaiting return to duty or onward evacuation; includes ICU hold in a Role 2 context.

mets.title: Conduct Casualty Evacuation
mets.description: Move casualties from the point of injury to successive echelons of care using designated CASEVAC and/or MEDEVAC assets.

mets.title: Provide Dental Services
mets.description: Maintain dental health readiness and deliver emergency dental services in support of the MAGTF.

mets.title: Maintain Dental Health Readiness
mets.description: Conduct routine and sustainment dental examinations and care to ensure personnel meet individual medical readiness standards.

mets.title: Provide Emergency Dental Services
mets.description: Deliver emergency dental treatment for maxillofacial trauma, acute dental pain, and infection.

mets.title: Conduct Medical Regulating
mets.description: Coordinate movement of patients between treatment facilities to match patient condition with capability available and optimize bed usage across the theater.
